subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Key Components
- **1 Game Board** with 4 advancement tracks (Science, Technology, Exploration, Military)
- **16 Civilization Mats** — each with a unique ability
- **5 Income Mats** (one per player)
- **5 Capital City Mats** with a 9×9 grid
- **18 Landmark Miniatures** — 3D buildings that fit on your capital city
- **43 Tapestry Cards** — played at the start of each income turn for ongoing benefits
- **48 Territory Tiles** — hexagonal tiles gained through Exploration
- **33 Tech Cards** — upgrades gained through the Science track
- **65 Income Buildings** (5 types, gained by completing track rows)
- **Resource tokens**: Food, Workers, Gold, Culture (coin-shaped)
- **Victory Point tokens** and player markers

subtopics.title: Initial Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Steps
1. Place the **game board** in the center
2. Place **landmark miniatures** on their designated spaces on the advancement tracks
3. Shuffle and deal **2 Civilization Mats** to each player — choose one, return the other
4. Each player takes an **Income Mat** and a **Capital City Mat**
5. Each player places their **player token** at the start of each of the 4 advancement tracks
6. Deal each player **3 Tapestry Cards** and **3 Tech Cards** (kept hidden)
7. Give each player starting resources based on your civilization's setup ability
8. Place **income buildings** in supply sorted by type
9. Shuffle **territory tiles** face-down in a supply

#### First Income Turn
All players simultaneously take their first income turn before the game begins (gain resources from the leftmost exposed column on your Income Mat).

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Two Types of Turns
On your turn you do **one** of two things:

**1. Advance Turn** — Pay resources to move up one space on any advancement track:
- Pay the cost shown on the next space (usually resources or VP)
- Gain the benefit of that space (landmarks, territory tiles, tech cards, etc.)
- If you reach a landmark space, take that miniature and place it on your Capital City Mat
- You may advance on any of the 4 tracks in any order

**2. Income Turn** — When you cannot or choose not to advance:
- **Play a Tapestry Card** from your hand (optional but important — gives ongoing bonuses)
- **Activate your Civilization ability** (some trigger on income turns)
- **Gain income** from all exposed symbols on your Income Mat
- You expose symbols by placing income buildings on your Income Mat

#### Important
- Players take turns independently — you might take your 3rd income turn while others are still advancing
- The game ends when ALL players have taken their **5th income turn**
- Each income turn, the income column you gain from moves right, giving more resources

subtopics.title: The Four Tracks
subtopics.content:
#### Science Track
- **Benefits**: Gain Tech Cards, upgrade Tech Cards for powerful abilities
- **Key landmark**: Research Lab
- Tech Cards have two sides — basic and upgraded (flip when you hit certain spaces)

#### Technology Track
- **Benefits**: Gain income buildings to place on your Income Mat (exposing income symbols)
- **Key landmark**: Tech Building
- Income buildings come in 5 types matching the 5 income columns

#### Exploration Track
- **Benefits**: Gain Territory Tiles to place on the main board, claim resources
- **Key landmark**: Landmark from exploration
- Territory tiles expand the map and can earn you control bonuses

#### Military Track
- **Benefits**: Conquer territories (flip opponent tiles), place outposts
- **Key landmark**: Armory
- Conquering lets you topple opponent territories adjacent to yours

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### When Does the Game End?
The game ends when **all players** have taken their 5th and final income turn.

#### Victory Point Sources
1. **VP tokens** earned during the game (from track spaces, tapestry cards, etc.)
2. **Landmark placement** — Score points for completed rows and columns on your Capital City Mat
3. **Capital City scoring** — Each completed row/column of your 9×9 grid scores points
4. **Tech Card bonuses** — Some upgraded tech cards give end-game VP
5. **Tapestry Card bonuses** — Some cards give VP based on game state
6. **Territory control** — Points for controlled territories on the main board

#### Winner
The player with the most VP wins. Ties broken by most resources remaining.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Key Strategic Principles

- **Don't spread too thin.** Focus on 2 advancement tracks rather than advancing equally on all 4. Going deep on fewer tracks earns better rewards.
- **Income buildings are crucial.** The Technology track gives you income buildings that dramatically increase your income each turn. Prioritize getting these early.
- **Tapestry cards are powerful.** Save strong tapestry cards for later income turns when they'll have more impact. Some tapestry cards are worth 10+ VP.
- **Fill your Capital City.** Landmarks and income buildings placed on your Capital City Mat score VP for completed rows and columns. Plan your placement carefully.
- **Watch your opponents' tracks.** Landmarks on the tracks are first-come, first-served. If someone is racing you on a track, decide if it's worth competing.
- **Use your Civilization ability.** Each civilization has a unique power — build your strategy around it rather than ignoring it.
- **Resource management matters.** Don't burn all resources early. You need them for all 5 eras. Gold is wild — save it for when you're short on a specific resource.

#### Common Mistakes
- Taking income turns too early (you lose potential advancement turns)
- Ignoring the Technology track (income buildings compound your gains)
- Not placing landmarks strategically on Capital City grid
